observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant effect the actual cash transfers and adjustments for the settlement of the inter-fund receivables and payables totaling ₱1,772,961.19; and b. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to ensure the prompt settlement and elimination of inter-fund receivables and payables in order to present the correct consolidated Statement of Financial Position at yearend.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented. The inter-fund transfer for the ₱1,772,961.19 effect was effected.
observations.reason: Not all inter-fund receivables and payables were settled and the Accounting Office overlooked to effect the settlement and elimination of all inter-fund consolidated receivables and payables at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, actual cash transfer of unexpended LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE from GF to TF books at yearend be effected by the Municipal Treasurer, and that appropriate accounting entries be drawn by the Municipal Accountant for the transfer of unexpended QRF and MOOE.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented.
observations.reason: No action taken. (Reiterated in Part II of this Report) The unexpended QRF and MOOE for CY 2024 were not transferred to TF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require: "b. The Municipal Treasurer to employ other collection strategies to persuade the afore-stated private firms to pay their taxes."
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to collect from other private firms. Although there is a court decision, it only exempted Smart Communications machineries from payment of RPT.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the following measures: a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of the local roads and prepare a report thereof.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented.
observations.reason: The LGU has an existing Inventory Committee, however, conduct of inventory was not completed during the year due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
1.5. We recommended and Management agreed that, hereafter, the Municipal
Accountant record the remittance of the GSIS loan amortizations and premium
contributions withheld from the salaries of employees only upon actual
payment/transfer to the GSIS.

2.6. We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the Municipal
Accountant make sure that RCDs are promptly recorded in the books of
accounts in order to provide accurate financial statements.

3.6. We recommended and it was agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer:

a. Effect the transfer of unexpended LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE from GF to TF
books amounting to ₱2,006,945.95 with the following accounting journal
entries:
GF Books (MDRRMF):
Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRMF to TF 2,006,945.95
Cash in Bank 2,006,945.95
To record transfer of Unspent QRF and MOOE to TF books.

TF Books:
Cash in Bank 2,006,945.95
Trust Liability-DRRMF 2,006,945.95
To record receipt of unspent QRF and MOOE from GF.

b. Henceforth, ensure the actual cash transfer of unexpended LDRRMF-QRF
and MOOE at yearend.

4.4. We recommended and Management agreed to implement the above-stated
recommendations; otherwise, inaction may result in further damage to the
projects and loss of public funds.

4.5. It was further reiterated that Management, specifically the Municipal Engineer,
ensure the adequate supervision and monitoring of all infrastructure projects
under his office.

5.5. We recommended and Management agreed to fully comply with the prescribed
format of the DV by incorporating the required certification of the official
directly responsible for the expenditures under Box A to ensure accountability and
transparency.

6.3. We recommended and Management agreed to issue collection/ demand letter
to the liable person in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a. Trace back and identify from the accounting records the unreconciled difference of ₱1,099,383.46 and draw the appropriate corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts; b. Set-up and maintain separate subsidiary ledger for each depository bank account; and c. Prepare separate Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for each bank account maintained by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The Accounting Office resolved the unreconciled difference.
observations.reason: Management complied with the recommendation. Separate monthly BRS have been prepared for each bank account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipal Accountant draw the appropriate Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to revert the stale checks after proper verification in order to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts in the Municipality’s books of accounts and henceforth, ensure that appropriate adjusting and reversing entries are promptly prepared for any stale checks; and b. The Municipal Treasurer give notices to payees at least one month before their check becomes stale to avoid the same circumstance in the future.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The Municipal Accountant prepared the appropriate JEVs to revert the stale checks.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer gave notices to payees at least one month before their check becomes stale.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) submit to the DILG for review the adjusted GPB for any changes in the original GPB pursuant to Item C.7(2) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.
observations.reason: The Municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the necessary Municipal Accountant set up the 20% DF in the e-NGAS software, record related transactions thereat, and generate reports of the special account therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office set-up the subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enforce the settlement of the remaining finding. (A related finding is presented in Part II of this Report).
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented. Management sent demand letters to person liable for the unsettled disallowance of ₱34,000.00.
observations.reason: The person liable is employed with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and it was agreed that henceforth, the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS)  ensure compliance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 on the preparation of the GPB and GAD AR.
observations.managementAction: (A related finding is presented in Part II of this Report). The MLGU streamlined PPAs more than the required amount.
observations.reason: The GFPS was not able to inform the DILG of the adjusted GPB for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review and consider the reversion of the stale checks before the year-end closing of books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reverted several of the stale checks.
observations.reason: Few stale checks totaling ₱82,657.80 were not yet reverted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management enforce the settlement of the remaining disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to the persons liable. The remaining uncollected disallowance as of December 31, 2023 amounted to ₱34,000.00.
observations.reason: The person liable is not already employed with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accounant effect the actual cash transfers and adjustments for the settlement of the inter-fund receivables and payables totaling ₱1,772,961.19; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to ensure the prompt settlement and elimination of inter-fund receivables and payables in order to present the correct consolidated Statement of Financial Position at yearend.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Trace back and identify from the accounting records  the unreconciled difference of ₱1,099,383.46 and draw the appropriate  corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts;
Set-up and maintain separate subsidiary ledger for each depository bank account; and
Prepare separate BRS for each bank account maintained by the Municipality.

We recommended and it was agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant draw the appropriate Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to revert the stale checks after proper verification in order to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts in the the Municipality’s books of accounts and henceforth, ensure that appropriate adjusting and reversing entries are promptly prepared for any stale checks; and
The Municipal Treasurer give notices to payees at least one month before their check becomes stale to avoid the same circumstance in the future.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, actual cash transfer of unexpended LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE from GF to TF books at yearend be effected by the Municipal Treasurer, and that appropriate accounting entries be drawn by the Municipal Accountant for the transfer of unexpended QRF and MOOE.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS)  submit to the DILG for review the adjusted GPB for any changes in the original GPB pursuant to Item C.7(2) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant set up the 20% DF in the e-NGAS software, record related transactions thereat, and generate reports of the special account therefrom.

We commended Management for complying with its afore-stated obligations to BIR and other GOCCs and recommended that Management continue to withhold and remit taxes, premium contributions and loan obligations timely.
We recommended that Management continue enforcing the settlement of the disallowances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take-up the proposed adjusting journal entries presented in the Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) to drop from the PPE accounts the property items below the ₱50,000.00 threshold, and adjust the misclassifications of infrastructure assets.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant dropped from the PPE accounts property items below the ₱50,000.00 threshold and adjsuted the misclassified infrastructure assets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Accounting Unit agreed to:

Take up the  Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) presented in the AOM to derecognize the Work/Zoo Animals transferred to different Barangays; and

Furnish the Barangays with the JEV as their basis in recognizing the assets in their books of account.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Unit took up the JEV presented in the AOM to derecognize the Work/Zoo Animals transferred to different Barangays; and

Furnish the Barangays with the JEV as their basis in recognizing the assets in their books of account.

The Accounting Unit took up the JEV presented in the AOM to derecognize the Work/Zoo Animals.

The Barangays were furnished of the JEV that the Accounting Unit recorded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require:

The Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to set up the correct RPT-Basic/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT-Basic/SET Income in CY 2023 based on the total assessed value of all taxable real properties; and

The Municipal Treasurer to employ other collection strategies to persuade the afore-stated private firms to pay their taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have drawn a JEV to set up the correct RPT-Basic/SET and Deferred RPT-Basic/SET Income.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to collect from other private firms. Although   there is a court decision, it only exempted Smart Communications machineries from payment of RPT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to submit written explanations/justifications on the grant of Gratuity Pay to JOs/COS without an approved supplemental budget.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted a written justification together with an approved supplemental budget for the gratuity pay of Job Orders/Contract of Service Personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Development Council to prepare and submit an updated LDIP and a supplemental AIP for the six PPAs to the Sannguniang Bayan for their appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Development Council prepared and submitted an updated LDIP which insluded the six PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to drop from the PPE accounts the properties costing less than ₱15,000.00; 


Require the Property Custodian to replace the existing Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) issued to the users with the Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS); and

Henceforth, record all properties costing less than ₱15,000.00 as inventory upon acquisition and expense upon issuance of the ICS.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant dropped from the PPE Accounts all semi-expendable property costing less than ₱15,000.00. 

The Property Custodian replaced all existing PARs with ICS to all issued inventoriable items.

Semi-expendable property costing less than ₱15,000.00 were recorded as inventory upon acquistion and expensed upon issuance of ICS.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the following measures:

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of the local roads and prepare a report thereof; and


Enjoin the Department Heads concerned to comply with the requirements through preparation and submission of the reports/data needed.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU has an existing Inventory Committee, However, conduct of inventory was not completed during the year.

Department Heads concerned have  submitted the required data.
observations.reason: Conduct of inventory during the year cannot be completed due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the – 

“b.Municipal Engineer and his staff to supervise closely all infrastructure projects to ensure that these are implemented strictly in accordance with approved plans and specifications; and

“c.Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to  monitor timely completion of projects in accordance with the project duration contained in the contract agreement.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer closely monitors his staff for the strict implementation of projects.

The MPDC exerted efforts to monitor infratructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to review and consider the reversion of the outstanding checks before the year end closing of books.

Infrastructure assets were not provided with residual value.




Adjusting entries were drawn to adjust the erroneous depreciation of infrastructure assets.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the BAC should require the local bidders/suppliers to include the taxes and other government costs in their bid price/quotation pursuant to Section 32.2.2, IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid irregular expenditures.
observations.managementAction: BAC has required the local bidders/suppliers to include taxes and other government costs in their bid price/quotation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to require the Accounting Office to stop providing for withholding taxes on reimbursement of expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office stopped the practice of adding the withholding taxes on reimbursement of expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 
     
disclose details of the Road Network account using the pro-forma disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant included the pro-forma disclosure of the Road Network account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management should enforce the settlement of the remaining disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The disallowances were partially settled in CY 2021. The remaining disallowances as of December 31, 2021 totaled ₱46,000.00.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in Part II of this report

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

“b. require the Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents such as Memorandum of Agreement or equivalent documents to determine the specific purpose of the fund transfers. This will assist the local officials in coming up with an informed decision whether to remit unexpended balances of P962,069.20 to the source agencies, or request their approval for its use for other purposes; and 

“c. Subsequently, require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund the unexpended balances of fund transfers on completed projects to source agencies.
observations.managementAction:

The LGU has refunded/returned the unexpended balances of completed projects.

create an Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local roads and prepare a report thereof; and



enjoin the Department Heads concerned to comply with the requirements through preparation and submission of the reports/data needed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the-

Contractor to remove and replace the defective pavements on the Bebley-Manaan Core Local Road Project;

Municipal Engineer and his staff to supervise closely all infrastructure projects to ensure that these are implemented strictly in accordance with approved plans and specifications; and

Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor the timely completion of projects in accordance with the project duration contained in the contract agreement.

record the liquidated damages as Miscellaneous Income which shall be used only through an  appropriation;  and
submit justification and stop using the liquidated damages for the payment of  BAC Members’ honoraria.

We have recommended that the BAC should require the local bidders/suppliers to include the taxes and other government costs in their bid price/quotations pursuant to Section 32.2.2, IRR of RA 9184 to avoid irregular expenditures.
We have recommended that the Accounting Office should stop providing for withholding taxes on reimbursement of expenditures.

We have recommended that Management enforce the settlement of the remaining disallowances.

the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to comply with Section 11(h) of NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume I by reconciling their records of appropriations, allotment, obligations and balances at the end of each month as basis for the preparation of the SCBAA; and

the Municipal Accountant to prepare a reconciliation of the actual amounts per SCBAA and financial statements using the format provided by COA Circular No. 2016-004.

The Municipal Accountant prepared the reconciliation of the actual amounts per SCBAA and financial statements.

a. include in the Notes to Financial Statements of the succeeding years the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
b. disclose details of the Road Network account using the pro-forma disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

a. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to comply with Section 11(h) of NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume I by reconciling their records of appropriations, allotment, obligations and balances at the end of each month as basis for the preparation of the SCBAA; and

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry to debit
the Due to NGAs account for the cost of completed projects amounting to
P7,800,000.00 and debit Government Equity, where appropriate;
b. require the Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents such
as Memorandum of Agreement or equivalent documents to determine the
specific purpose of the fund transfers. This will assist the local officials in
coming up with an informed decision whether to remit unexpended
balances of P962,069.20 to the source agencies, or request their approval
for its use for other purposes;
c. subsequently, require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund
the unexpended balances of fund transfers on completed projects to
source agencies.

2.7 We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to consider the
following measures:
a. create an Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local
roads and prepare a report thereof;
b. enjoin the Department Heads concerned to comply with the
requirements through preparation and submission of the reports/data
needed; and,
c. require the Municipal Accountant to completely record all road
networks, contained in the RPI as of December 31, 2015, by the end of
CY 2019, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

3.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the members of
the Finance Committee to collect the remaining unsettled disallowances, in
accordance with the rules on the settlement of accounts.

4.4 We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
coordinate with the MDRRMO to regularly prepare and promptly submit
the Monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF.

5.7 We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to include in
the Notes to Financial Statements of succeeding years the amount and
details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, in accordance with
COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommend that the Plan be enforced strictly and introduce other strategies to suit present conditions in managing solid and other wastes inorder that clean air and the healthy environment are preserved for the future.

We encourage the GAD Focal Person to attend GAD trainings including ways of gathering and accomplishing the data base and in conducting gender analysis of their PPAs through the administration of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG).
We encourage management and concerned personnel to continue doing well their job.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to:
a) limit payroll deductions and follow the order of preference
provided in the GAA to ensure that all employees’ take home pay
shall not be less than the minimum allowed by law;
b) cease from being a withholding agent for loan obligations of
employees in institutions not allowed in the provisions above; and
c) hold the withholding agents/officials responsible for allowing the
net take home pay of the employees lesser than that allowed by
law.

This was an audit observation in the past year and we recommended that
management adopt internal control measures in its payroll system by
considering the issuance of checks or availing of Land Bank’s Automated
Teller Machines (ATM), in paying the salaries and wages of the
municipality’s officers and employees.

We have reiterated our recommendation in the past year that
management consider the availment of Land Bank’s Automated
Teller Machines (ATM), in paying the salaries and wages of the
municipality’s officers and employees, since payment by check is
unacceptable to most of them.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
coordinate with the MDRRMO-Designate for the regular preparation
and submission of the Monthly Report on the Utilization of
LDRRMF.

We have recommended that Management adopt internal control measures in its payroll system by considering the issuance of checks or availing of LandBank’s Automated Teller Machines (ATM), in paying salaries and wages of the Municipality’s officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of coordinating with LandBank for its ATM or Payroll Account System.
observations.reason: they target to be fully implemented by July 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to:
 Limit payroll deductions and follow the order of preference provided in the GAA to ensure that all employees’ take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed by law;
 Cease from being a withholding agent for loan obligations of employees in institutions not allowed in the provisions above; and
 Hold the withholding agents/officials responsible for allowing the net take home pay of the employees lesser than that allowed by law.
observations.managementAction: Management Partially implemented the recommendation (Please see a modified observation in Part II of this report) starting June 2017.
Management Fully followed up the approval of the ESWM Plan.
observations.reason: The LGU has been consistent in conducting IEC. Some barangays have limited funds for an MRF. The Tinoc Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan is not yet approved but LGU activities were done in fulfillment of the submitted Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to-
 Provide the MDRRM Office with funds for its office operations; and
 Refrain from paying regular office MOOE and CO from the LDRRMF and strictly adhere with the guidelines set under Section 4.3 of JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management Fully Implemented the provision to provide the MDRRM Office with funds for its office operations. Partially Implemented the refrain from paying regular office MOOE and CO from the LDRRMF.

Management justified that the reasons for the delay were beyond their control such as Right of Way problems, insufficient funds and unspecified tourism projects. A surveying instrument was budgeted for procurement in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Some activities on Preliminary Engineering were conducted for CY 2017 projects.

observations.recommendation: We have commended Management for its efficiency in doing its function as collecting agent of the Bureau of Internal Revenue. We encourage management and concerned personnel to continue doing well their job.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to withhold and remit taxes to the BIR in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management-
 Fast track the completion of the two (2) bridges, to benefit the people and commuters of Tinoc;
 For forthcoming projects, conduct thorough preliminary surveys and involve non-government organizations in preparation of plans to ensure smooth project implementation; and
 Devise strategies to properly accomplish infrastructure and other BUB projects on time, to avail of additional incentives.
observations.managementAction: Only the Bebley Bridge remained unfinished. Although funding was provided, the contractor was not able to finish it as of year-end. A surveying instrument was budgeted for CY 2018. The latest BUB projects were implemented satisfactorily.
observations.reason: Please see additional related finding in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the Local School Board to convene regularly and comply strictly with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure that Special Education Fund is budgeted and fully utilized in promoting quality education in every public school at the district level.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had allocated assistance to schools from the SEF, aside from the amount appropriated from the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require all persons liable to settle the disallowances within six months from receipt of the Notice of Disallowance, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 or make a request from the COA Legal Services Sector for authority to settle disallowances on installment basis.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of disallowed personnel (benefits were made thru salary deduction and report.) targeted to be fully settled as of December 2018 except for the disallowance on the purchased motor vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require all elected officials and heads of offices to undergo capacity-building programs on GAD.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that most capacity-building programs were incorporated in other activities of the municipality.
observations.reason: No specific training was conducted on HGDG.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the Supply Officer to prepare henceforth annually, an updated Inventory report of all LGU property and reconcile it with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Supply Officer conducted an updated inventory of all LGU property and reconcile it with accounting records.
observations.reason: An updated Inventory report was submitted for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive collect immediately the bank charges of P112,000.00 from persons responsible and deposit it back to its depository bank.
observations.managementAction: Management filed a request for reconsideration of the Land Bank of the Philippines. The disallowed amount was settled by Land Bank of the Philippines. P78,200.00 as of December 31, 2017, with a remaining balance of P34,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer to:
 monitor the timely completion of infrastructure projects;
 Compute correctly the liquidated damages for the above completed projects and require the contractors to pay the same; and
 Enforce deduction of liquidated damages from the final billings of contractors who are in similar situation.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. More projects were completed on time in CY 2017 as compared to prior years. Liquidated damages were computed from the contractors of delayed projects and deducted from the claims of contractors with delayed project completion.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management –
 direct the Department Heads and their permanent regular staff to perform efficiently the duties assigned to their office and not to depend on job order personnel.
 Decrease the budget for wages of job order personnel of the different departments and instead use the amount to provide basic services to majority of the constituents.
 Limit the hiring of personnel through contracts of service, only for essential activities of approved local projects; and
 Specify in the appointments the project and activities to be undertaken which are measurable.
observations.managementAction: Management Partially implemented the recommendation. Management hired 142 personnel on Job Order basis in CY 2017. The Department Heads did not prepare bloated budgets. The expenditure decreased since the number of personnel hired decreased in CY 2017. The actual works done were reflected in the Accomplishment Report attached to the Daily Time Record.
observations.reason: 142 personnel were still hired on job order basis in CY 2017; some recommendations were implemented while others were only partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management conduct thorough detailed preliminary engineering activities including exact project location before starting the procurement process of future infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Some activities on Preliminary Engineering were conducted for CY 2017 projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management inform the Contractor to post additional performance security to fully cover the period of implementation and final acceptance of the project.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management -
 Enact ordinances and executive orders for the segregation of solid waste at source, enforce its implementation and impose penalties to violators.
 Promote re-use and recycling of waste materials to reduce the volume of garbage to be collected and construct Materials Recovery Facilities (MRF).
 Implement strictly the Tinoc Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan, if approved.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The Sangguniang Bayan had enacted an ordinance endorsing the Solid Waste Management Plan. Re-use and recycling of waste materials are being promoted in the locality. The Tinoc Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan is not yet approved but LGU activities were done in fulfillment of the submitted Plan.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the Local School Board to convene and comply strictly with the provisions of RA No. 7160 to ensure that the Special Education Fund is budgeted and fully utilized for the promotion of quality education in every public school.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented.
observations.reason: Please see related finding in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the –
 Municipal Engineer and his staff to supervise closely all infrastructure projects to ensure that these are implemented strictly in accordance with the approved plans and specifications;
 Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor the timely completion of projects in accordance with the project duration contained in the contract agreement; and
 Technical Working Group (TWG) of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to judiciously and accurately compute amounts before these are recommended for award.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented.
observations.reason: See related finding in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
 Instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast tract the implementation of the projects and supervise them strictly to ensure that they are constructed in accordance with the approved plans and specifications; and
 Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to review regularly the status of implementation of the Annual Investment Plan, assist in the timely execution of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) and submit periodic reports to the Local Chief Executive, as his basis in taking appropriate actions.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The MPDO had been monitoring implementation of the Annual Investment Plan and the project activities.
Fully Implemented. The Department Heads did not prepare bloated budgets. Heads submitted attainable budgets. An attainable budget for CY 2018 was prepared with the assistance of the LFC.
No comprehensive corrective actions implemented; some departments advised but full reforms not effected.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management adopt internal control measures in its payroll system by considering the issuance of checks or availing of Land Bank’s Automated Teller Machines (ATM), in paying the salaries and wages of the municipality’s officers and employees.

We have recommended and management agreed to:
 a) limit payroll deductions and follow the order of preference provided in the GAA to ensure that all employees’ take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed by law;
 b) cease from being a withholding agent for loan obligations of employees in institutions not allowed in the provisions above; and
 c) hold the withholding agents/officials responsible for allowing the net take home pay of the employees lesser than that allowed by law.

We have recommended and management agreed to-
 a) provide the MDRRM office with funds for its office operations; and
 b) refrain from paying regular office MOOE and CO from the LDRRMF and strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 4.3 of the JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We have recommended that Management :
 Instruct the Supply Officer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the documents of the donated ambulance, as her basis of recording it in the books;
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the cost of the donated ambulance, based on the documents coming from the donor; and
 Require the Supply Officer to prepare henceforth annually, an updated Inventory Report of all LGU property and reconcile it with accounting records.
observations.managementAction:
Management fully complied with the audit recommendation.
Management fully complied with the audit recommendation.
Not implemented.

observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented.
Management filed a request for reconsideration with Land Bank of the Philippines.
Fully implemented.
Fully implemented.
observations.reason: No NSF or overdrafts during the year.

observations.managementAction: Conducted IEC; segregation and composting now implemented in the municipality; constructed MRFs and closed open dump site; MSWMB activated; implementation of SWM Plan partially implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to-
a) provide the MDRRM office with funds for its office operations; and
b) refrain from paying regular office MOOE and CO from the LDRRMF and strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 4.3 of the JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to provide MDRRM office with funds for operations and to refrain from charging regular MOOE and CO to LDRRMF.
Municipal Engineer acknowledged delays and indicated inadequate manpower and procurement issues; Management exerted efforts to implement projects; some projects completed from prior years.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer coordinate and seek the concurrence of DAR Officials on the Programs of Work, Variation Orders and other related documents of the on-going projects, in conformity with the MOA and submit these for audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented and submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management implement the following measures:
• Submit certification that detailed preliminary engineering activities were conducted with the corresponding evidences; and,
• Conduct thorough detailed preliminary engineering activities including exact project location before starting the procurement process of future infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented; some certifications and evidence provided; need to conduct more thorough preliminary engineering.
Management submitted clarifications and additional documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to inform the Contractor to post additional performance security to fully cover the period of implementation until its completion and final acceptance by management of the project.
observations.managementAction: Management informed contractor; additional performance security not fully posted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that personnel of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office, in coordination with barangay officials, choose fairly the clients for assistance, in accordance with DSWD rules and guidelines on the program. Further, enact a resolution or executive order to set clear and systematic rules which are aligned with the DSWD guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Personnel complied and directives issued to choose clients fairly and align with DSWD guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to check and verify the correctness of the Fund Utilization Reports before affixing her signature on the document.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant now checks and verifies the Fund Utilization Reports prior to signing.
Conducted IEC; segregation and composting now implemented; promoted reuse and recycling; MRFs constructed; open dump site closed; MSWMB activated; implementation of SWM Plan partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to require the Local School Board to convene and comply strictly with the provisions of RA No. 7160 to ensure that the Special Education Fund is budgeted and fully utilized for the promotion of quality education in every public school.
observations.managementAction: Local School Board convened and some utilization undertaken; partial compliance to RA No. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the members of the GAD Focal Point System to prepare realizable objectives/targets and monitor their full attainment during the year.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented; GAD Focal Point prepared objectives but monitoring and full attainment not consistently done.

observations.recommendation: We have commended Management for the continuous effort in enforcing the settlement of disallowances. We have also recommended that Management practice prudence in utilization of government funds and property, to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management continued efforts to collect disallowances; partial settlements effected.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management-
• Encourage the members of the MDRRMC to perform their duties and responsibilities;
• Fast tract the rehabilitation, repair of public facilities/infrastructure and the provision of assistance to affected individuals; and
• Comply with the provisions of R.A. No.10121, especially on the utilization of the Quick Response Fund.
observations.managementAction: Partial actions taken; MDRRMC members encouraged; some rehabilitation and assistance provided; compliance improved but not fully achieved.
Municipal Engineer and staff required to prepare proper Programs of Work; closer supervision instituted; MPDC monitoring enhanced; TWG/BAC advised to improve computations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management :
• Instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast tract the implementation of the projects and supervise them strictly to ensure that they are constructed in accordance with the approved plans and specifications; and
• Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to review regularly the status of implementation of the Annual Investment Plan, assist in the timely execution of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) and submit periodic reports to the Local Chief Executive, as his basis in taking appropriate actions.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed Municipal Engineer to fast track implementation; MPDC to review and monitor AIP; some projects remain delayed.
